channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: SPARK: Jailbreaking T2V Models by Synergistically Prompting Auditory and Recontextualized Knowledge
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SPARK, a jailbreak framework that crafts benign-looking prompts to induce text-to-video (T2V) models to produce semantically unsafe videos while preserving original blocked intent.
  • Prompts are modular: neutral scene anchors (surface plausibility), latent auditory triggers (textual innocuous-sounding audio cues exploiting audio-visual co-occurrence), and stylistic modulators (cinematic directives to amplify/stabilize effects).
  • Attack generation is framed as constrained optimization over the prompt space and solved via a guided search; evaluated on 7 T2V models, showing ~+23% average attack success improvement on commercial models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zonghao Ying', 'Moyang Chen', 'Nizhang Li', 'Zhiqiang Wang', 'Wenxin Zhang', 'Quanchen Zou', 'Zonglei Jing', 'Aishan Liu', 'Xianglong Liu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'prompt-injection', 'adversarial-attack', 'multimodal-security', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Restoring Linguistic Grounding in VLA Models via Train-Free Attention Recalibration
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a failure mode in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models called "linguistic blindness", where models ignore contradictory language instructions and follow visual priors.
  • Introduces ICBench, a diagnostic benchmark that injects out-of-distribution contradictory instructions into LIBERO tasks to probe language-action coupling.
  • Finds that multiple representative VLA architectures often execute visually plausible actions despite logically impossible instructions.
  • Proposes IGAR (Instruction-Guided Attention Recalibration), a train-free, inference-time mechanism that rebalances attention to restore instruction influence; demonstrates reduced erroneous executions across 30 tasks and on a real Franka robot.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ninghao Zhang', 'Bin Zhu', 'Shijie Zhou', 'Jingjing Chen']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'safety', 'benchmarks', 'inference-time defense', 'vision-language-action']

item.title: Latent Transfer Attack: Adversarial Examples via Generative Latent Spaces
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes LTA (Latent Transfer Attack): an adversarial attack that optimizes perturbations in the latent space of a pretrained Stable Diffusion VAE rather than pixel space.
  • Incorporates pixel-space ℓ∞ budget after decoding, Expectation Over Transformations (randomized resizing/interpolation/cropping) for robustness, and periodic latent Gaussian smoothing to reduce artifacts.
  • Demonstrates strong transferability across CNN and vision-transformer targets, producing spatially coherent, predominantly low-frequency perturbations that differ qualitatively from pixel-space attacks.
  • Highlights pretrained generative latent spaces as structured domains for adversarial optimization and robustness evaluation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Eitan Shaar', 'Ariel Shaulov', 'Yalcin Tur', 'Gal Chechik', 'Ravid Shwartz-Ziv']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'transfer-attacks', 'generative-latent-space', 'robustness', 'vision']

item.title: Word-Anchored Temporal Forgery Localization
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces WAFL (Word-Anchored Temporal Forgery Localization), reframing temporal forgery localization as discrete word-level binary classification rather than boundary regression or continuous frame-level detection.
  • Proposes a Forensic Feature Realignment (FFR) module to map pre-trained semantic representations into a discriminative forensic manifold, enabling lightweight classifiers to detect forged words efficiently.
  • Designs an Artifact-Centric Asymmetric (ACA) loss to address extreme class imbalance by suppressing dominant authentic gradients and prioritizing subtle forensic artifact signals.
  • Reports substantial improvements over state-of-the-art localization performance across in- and cross-dataset evaluations while reducing parameter count and computational cost.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tianyi Wang', 'Xi Shao', 'Harry Cheng', 'Yinglong Wang', 'Mohan Kankanhalli']
  • Tags: ['audio forgery detection', 'temporal localization', 'deepfake/forensics', 'class-imbalance loss']

item.title: Lyapunov Probes for Hallucination Detection in Large Foundation Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Frames hallucination detection in LLMs/MLLMs as a dynamical-systems stability problem, modeling factual knowledge as stable equilibria in representation space.
  • Introduces Lyapunov Probes: lightweight networks trained with derivative-based stability constraints to enforce monotonic confidence decay under input perturbations.
  • Uses systematic perturbation analysis and a two-stage training process to distinguish stable (factual) regions from unstable, hallucination-prone regions.
  • Evaluated across multiple datasets and models, reporting consistent improvements over existing hallucination-detection baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bozhi Luan', 'Gen Li', 'Yalan Qin', 'Jifeng Guo', 'Yun Zhou', 'Faguo Wu', 'Hongwei Zheng', 'Wenjun Wu', 'Zhaoxin Fan']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'defense', 'robustness', 'dynamical-systems', 'safety-probes']

item.title: BlackMirror: Black-Box Backdoor Detection for Text-to-Image Models via Instruction-Response Deviation
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BlackMirror, a training-free, black-box framework to detect backdoors in text-to-image models.
  • Introduces MirrorMatch to align visual patterns with instructions for detecting semantic deviations, and MirrorVerify to test the stability of those deviations across varied prompts.
  • Designed as a plug-and-play module for Model-as-a-Service deployments and evaluated across diverse backdoor attacks, including cases with visually diverse triggered generations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Feiran Li', 'Qianqian Xu', 'Shilong Bao', 'Zhiyong Yang', 'Xilin Zhao', 'Xiaochun Cao', 'Qingming Huang']
  • Tags: ['backdoor-detection', 'black-box', 'text-to-image', 'model-security', 'defense']

item.title: Margin and Consistency Supervision for Calibrated and Robust Vision Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Margin and Consistency Supervision (MaCS), a training regularizer combining a hinge-squared margin penalty on logits and a KL-based consistency loss between clean and mildly perturbed views.
  • Provides theoretical analysis linking increased logit margin and reduced local sensitivity to improved generalization and a provable robustness-radius bound that scales with the margin-to-sensitivity ratio.
  • Empirically improves calibration (ECE, NLL) and robustness to common corruptions across multiple image classification benchmarks and backbones (CNNs and ViTs) without extra data or architectural changes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Salim Khazem']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'calibration', 'consistency-regularization', 'margin-regularization', 'vision']

item.title: Post Fusion Bird's Eye View Feature Stabilization for Robust Multimodal 3D Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Post Fusion Stabilizer (PFS), a lightweight module that refines intermediate bird's-eye view (BEV) features from existing camera-LiDAR fusion 3D detectors to improve robustness to domain shift and sensor corruptions.
  • PFS stabilizes feature statistics, suppresses regions affected by sensor degradation, and uses residual correction to restore weakened cues while acting as a near-identity transformation to preserve baseline performance.
  • Demonstrates improved robustness on nuScenes under various failure modes (e.g., +1.2% camera dropout robustness, +4.4% mAP in low-light) with a small parameter overhead (~3.3M).
  • Designed to be a drop-in post-fusion module that does not require modifying or retraining the original fusion architecture.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Trung Tien Dong', 'Dev Thakkar', 'Arman Sargolzaei', 'Xiaomin Lin']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'sensor-failure tolerance', 'multimodal fusion', 'autonomous driving', 'BEV feature stabilization']

item.title: From Decoupled to Coupled: Robustness Verification for Learning-based Keypoint Detection with Joint Specifications
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a coupled robustness verification framework for heatmap-based keypoint detectors that bounds joint deviations across all keypoints, capturing interdependencies and downstream task requirements.
  • Formulates verification as a falsification problem solved via a mixed-integer linear program (MILP) that combines reachable heatmap sets with a polytope encoding of joint deviation constraints; infeasibility yields a sound robustness certificate, feasibility yields counterexamples.
  • Empirical results show higher verified rates and effectiveness under strict error thresholds where prior decoupled (per-keypoint) verification methods are overly conservative or fail.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xusheng Luo', 'Changliu Liu']
  • Tags: ['robustness verification', 'formal verification', 'adversarial robustness', 'MILP', 'computer vision']

item.title: Agent Tools Orchestration Leaks More: Dataset, Benchmark, and Mitigation
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a new privacy vulnerability (Tools Orchestration Privacy Risk, TOP-R) where multi-tool LLM agents aggregate non-sensitive fragments from different tools to infer sensitive information.
  • Provides a formal characterization of TOP-R (three necessary conditions), constructs TOP-Bench via a RISE pipeline with paired social-context scenarios, and introduces the H-Score to measure utility-safety trade-offs.
  • Empirically evaluates six state-of-the-art LLMs showing high leakage rates, analyzes root causes (privacy awareness gaps, reasoning overshoot, inference inertia), and proposes mitigation strategies; the best configuration (Dual-Constraint Privacy Enhancement) substantially improves H-Score.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuxuan Qiao', 'Dongqin Liu', 'Hongchang Yang', 'Wei Zhou', 'Songlin Hu']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'autonomous-agents', 'dataset', 'benchmark', 'defenses']

item.title: Sysformer: Safeguarding Frozen Large Language Models with Adaptive System Prompts
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Sysformer, a transformer that adapts the system prompt in the LLM input embedding space while keeping the target LLM frozen.
  • Trains Sysformer to steer responses toward refusal on harmful prompts and correct replies on safe prompts, improving safety without fine-tuning the LLM.
  • Evaluates on 5 LLMs and 2 benchmarks, showing large gains in refusal rates on harmful prompts and improved compliance on safe prompts; robust against various jailbreaking attacks.
  • Aims to provide a cheaper, deployable defense (adaptive system prompts) for safeguarding instruction-tuned LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kartik Sharma', 'Yiqiao Jin', 'Vineeth Rakesh', 'Yingtong Dou', 'Menghai Pan', 'Mahashweta Das', 'Srijan Kumar']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'jailbreaking', 'prompt-based safety', 'robustness', 'model hardening']

item.title: Fragile Thoughts: How Large Language Models Handle Chain-of-Thought Perturbations
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a taxonomy of 5 Chain-of-Thought (CoT) perturbation types (MathError, UnitConversion, Sycophancy, SkippedSteps, ExtraSteps) and injects these perturbations into intermediate reasoning steps.
  • Empirically evaluates 13 LLMs (≈3B to 1.5T parameters) on mathematical reasoning tasks to measure robustness to these perturbations and where in the chain they are injected.
  • Finds heterogeneous vulnerability patterns: MathError harms small models most but improves with scale; UnitConversion remains challenging across scales; ExtraSteps have minimal effect; Sycophancy and SkippedSteps cause modest-to-intermediate degradation.
  • Reports scaling relationships (power-law) and emphasizes implications for multi-stage reasoning pipelines, highlighting need for task-specific robustness assessments and mitigation strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ashwath Vaithinathan Aravindan', 'Mayank Kejriwal']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'adversarial-perturbation', 'chain-of-thought', 'evaluation', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Window-based Membership Inference Attacks Against Fine-tuned Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces WBC (Window-Based Comparison), a sliding-window, sign-based aggregation attack for membership inference against fine-tuned LLMs that compares token-sequence losses between target and reference models.
  • Ensembles binary votes from geometrically spaced window sizes to capture memorization signals from token-level to phrase-level, rather than relying on global-average loss.
  • Evaluates across eleven datasets, showing substantial improvements in AUC and 2–3x better detection rates at low false-positive thresholds, revealing privacy vulnerabilities in fine-tuned LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuetian Chen', 'Yuntao Du', 'Kaiyuan Zhang', 'Ashish Kundu', 'Charles Fleming', 'Bruno Ribeiro', 'Ninghui Li']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'privacy', 'attack', 'LLM memorization', 'adversarial evaluation']

item.title: SAHOO: Safeguarded Alignment for High-Order Optimization Objectives in Recursive Self-Improvement
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SAHOO, a framework of three safeguards to prevent alignment drift during recursive self-improvement: (i) Goal Drift Index (GDI) — a learned multi-signal detector, (ii) constraint preservation checks for safety-critical invariants, and (iii) regression-risk quantification to flag harmful improvement cycles.
  • Empirical evaluation on 189 tasks (code generation, mathematical reasoning, truthfulness) shows substantial quality gains (e.g., +18.3% on code, +16.8% on reasoning) while preserving constraints and maintaining low truthfulness violations; thresholds calibrated on a small validation set.
  • Analyzes the capability–alignment frontier, documenting rising alignment costs with further self-improvement and domain-specific tensions (e.g., fluency vs. factuality), making alignment preservation measurable and deployable.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subramanyam Sahoo', 'Aman Chadha', 'Vinija Jain', 'Divya Chaudhary']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'alignment', 'recursive self-improvement', 'safety monitoring', 'robustness']

item.title: Do Compact SSL Backbones Matter for Audio Deepfake Detection? A Controlled Study with RAPTOR
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents RAPTOR, a pairwise-gated fusion detector, to evaluate compact SSL backbones (HuBERT, WavLM) for audio deepfake detection across 14 cross-domain benchmarks.
  • Finds that multilingual HuBERT pre-training, rather than model scale, primarily drives cross-domain robustness—allowing ~100M models to match larger/commercial systems.
  • Introduces a test-time augmentation protocol using perturbation-based aleatoric uncertainty to reveal calibration differences not visible in standard metrics.
  • Reports that WavLM variants show overconfident miscalibration under perturbations, while iterative mHuBERT remains more stable, highlighting pre-training trajectory impact on reliable detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ajinkya Kulkarni', 'Sandipana Dowerah', 'Atharva Kulkarni', 'Tanel Alum\\"ae', 'Mathew Magimai Doss']
  • Tags: ['audio-deepfake-detection', 'robustness', 'self-supervised-learning', 'calibration', 'test-time-augmentation']

item.title: Proof-of-Guardrail in AI Agents and What (Not) to Trust from It
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Proof-of-Guardrail: a system using Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) to produce cryptographic attestations that a specific open-source guardrail ran when generating an agent response.
  • Implements the approach for OpenClaw agents and measures latency overhead and deployment costs.
  • Maintains developer model privacy while ensuring integrity of guardrail execution, and discusses practical limitations and attack scenarios (e.g., malicious developers actively jailbreaking or deceiving attestations).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xisen Jin', 'Michael Duan', 'Qin Lin', 'Aaron Chan', 'Zhenglun Chen', 'Junyi Du', 'Xiang Ren']
  • Tags: ['guardrails', 'TEE/attestation', 'verifiable safety', 'jailbreaking/attack analysis', 'deployment evaluation']

item.title: Towards Robust Retrieval-Augmented Generation Based on Knowledge Graph: A Comparative Analysis
item.description:
Summary

  • Uses the Retrieval-Augmented Generation Benchmark (RGB) to evaluate RAG systems across four scenarios: noise robustness, information integration, negative rejection, and counterfactual robustness.
  • Compares the RGB RAG baseline with GraphRAG, a knowledge-graph-based retrieval approach, and tests three GraphRAG customizations aimed at improving robustness.
  • Finds improvements over the RGB baseline and provides design insights for building more reliable RAG systems in real-world settings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hazem Amamou', "St\\'ephane Gagnon", 'Alan Davoust', 'Anderson R. Avila']
  • Tags: ['retrieval-augmented generation', 'knowledge-graph', 'robustness', 'defense', 'benchmarking']

item.title: The Fragility Of Moral Judgment In Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a perturbation framework to test stability/manipulability of LLM moral judgments using 2,939 real-world dilemmas and 129,156 model judgments across four LLMs.
  • Evaluates three perturbation families—surface edits, point-of-view shifts, and persuasion cues—and multiple evaluation protocols to measure flip rates and directional biases.
  • Finds surface noise yields low flip rates (~7.5%), POV shifts cause substantial instability (~24.3%), persuasion cues produce systematic directional shifts, and protocol choices (prompt scaffolding/order) dominate reproducibility.
  • Shows instability concentrates in morally ambiguous cases and warns that narrative form and presentation skill can co-produce moral outcomes, raising robustness, fairness, and reproducibility concerns.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tom van Nuenen', 'Pratik S. Sachdeva']
  • Tags: ['prompt-manipulation', 'adversarial-robustness', 'model-manipulation', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Safer Reasoning Traces: Measuring and Mitigating Chain-of-Thought Leakage in LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a model-agnostic framework for measuring token-level, risk-weighted PII leakage from Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning across 11 PII types with a hierarchical risk taxonomy and leakage-vs-budget curves.
  • Empirically evaluates open- and closed-source LLM families and shows CoT commonly increases PII leakage (especially high-risk categories), with leakage outcomes dependent on model family and reasoning budget.
  • Benchmarks lightweight inference-time gatekeepers (rule-based detector, TF-IDF + logistic regression, GLiNER NER, and LLM-as-judge) using risk-weighted metrics and finds no single method universally effective, motivating hybrid/style-adaptive defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Patrick Ahrend', 'Tobias Eder', 'Xiyang Yang', 'Zhiyi Pan', 'Georg Groh']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'PII leakage', 'chain-of-thought', 'defenses', 'benchmarking']

item.title: NOTAI.AI: Explainable Detection of Machine-Generated Text via Curvature and Feature Attribution
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes NOTAI.AI, a supervised, explainable detector for machine-generated text combining curvature-based signals, neural detector scores, stylometric and readability features.
  • Uses 17 interpretable features (including Conditional Probability Curvature and ModernBERT detector), aggregated via an XGBoost meta-classifier for human vs. AI text classification.
  • Applies SHAP for local/global feature attribution and translates attributions into natural-language rationales using an LLM explanation layer; deployed as an interactive web app with open-source code and demo.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Oleksandr Marchenko Breneur', 'Adelaide Danilov', 'Aria Nourbakhsh', 'Salima Lamsiyah']
  • Tags: ['Text-Generation Detection', 'Explainability', 'Model Forensics', 'Defensive Tools']

item.title: Fragile Thoughts: How Large Language Models Handle Chain-of-Thought Perturbations
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a taxonomy of 5 Chain-of-Thought (CoT) perturbation types (MathError, UnitConversion, Sycophancy, SkippedSteps, ExtraSteps) and injects these perturbations into intermediate reasoning steps.
  • Empirically evaluates 13 LLMs (≈3B to 1.5T parameters) on mathematical reasoning tasks to measure robustness to these perturbations and where in the chain they are injected.
  • Finds heterogeneous vulnerability patterns: MathError harms small models most but improves with scale; UnitConversion remains challenging across scales; ExtraSteps have minimal effect; Sycophancy and SkippedSteps cause modest-to-intermediate degradation.
  • Reports scaling relationships (power-law) and emphasizes implications for multi-stage reasoning pipelines, highlighting need for task-specific robustness assessments and mitigation strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ashwath Vaithinathan Aravindan', 'Mayank Kejriwal']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'adversarial-perturbation', 'chain-of-thought', 'evaluation', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Sysformer: Safeguarding Frozen Large Language Models with Adaptive System Prompts
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Sysformer, a transformer that adapts the system prompt in the LLM input embedding space while keeping the target LLM frozen.
  • Trains Sysformer to steer responses toward refusal on harmful prompts and correct replies on safe prompts, improving safety without fine-tuning the LLM.
  • Evaluates on 5 LLMs and 2 benchmarks, showing large gains in refusal rates on harmful prompts and improved compliance on safe prompts; robust against various jailbreaking attacks.
  • Aims to provide a cheaper, deployable defense (adaptive system prompts) for safeguarding instruction-tuned LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kartik Sharma', 'Yiqiao Jin', 'Vineeth Rakesh', 'Yingtong Dou', 'Menghai Pan', 'Mahashweta Das', 'Srijan Kumar']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'jailbreaking', 'prompt-based safety', 'robustness', 'model hardening']

item.title: ContextBench: Modifying Contexts for Targeted Latent Activation
item.description:
Summary

  • Formalizes 'context modification' as a class of methods to generate linguistically fluent inputs that activate specific latent features or elicit target behaviours in language models.
  • Introduces ContextBench, a benchmark to assess elicitation strength and linguistic fluency for targeted activation and safety-related use cases.
  • Evaluates state-of-the-art methods, showing trade-offs between activation effectiveness and fluency.
  • Proposes enhanced Evolutionary Prompt Optimisation (EPO) variants using LLM assistance and diffusion-model inpainting, achieving improved balance between elicitation and fluency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Robert Graham', 'Edward Stevinson', 'Leo Richter', 'Alexander Chia', 'Joseph Miller', 'Joseph Isaac Bloom']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'targeted-elicitation', 'benchmarking', 'model-safety', 'jailbreaking']

item.title: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Answer-Then-Check (Reasoned Safety Alignment, ReSA): models first generate an answer 'in thought' then evaluate its safety before producing a final response, aimed at mitigating jailbreaks.
  • Introduces the ReSA dataset (80K samples) for training this safety reasoning behavior and shows fine-tuned models reach a strong safety-vs-utility Pareto frontier with reduced over-refusal.
  • Evaluations indicate the method preserves general reasoning (MMLU, MATH500, HumanEval), outperforms post-hoc detectors for safe completion, and can be data-efficient (≈500 samples can approach full-data performance).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chentao Cao', 'Xiaojun Xu', 'Bo Han', 'Hang Li']
  • Tags: ['jailbreak defense', 'safety alignment', 'safety dataset', 'robustness training']

item.title: Temporal Misalignment Attacks against Multimodal Perception in Autonomous Driving
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces DejaVu, an attack that manipulates in-vehicle time to create subtle temporal misalignment between camera and LiDAR streams, degrading multimodal fusion (MMF) perception.
  • Shows task-specific sensitivities: single-frame LiDAR delay can reduce car detection mAP by up to 88.5%, while a three-frame camera delay can drop car MOTA by 73%.
  • Validates feasibility with an automotive Ethernet hardware-in-the-loop testbed and end-to-end Autoware simulation, demonstrating real-world impacts (collisions, phantom braking).
  • Provides code/artifacts for reproducibility and evaluates across models and datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Md Hasan Shahriar', 'Md Mohaimin Al Barat', 'Harshavardhan Sundar', 'Ning Zhang', 'Naren Ramakrishnan', 'Y. Thomas Hou', 'Wenjing Lou']
  • Tags: ['temporal misalignment', 'multimodal fusion', 'autonomous driving', 'adversarial attack', 'sensor integrity']

item.title: Federated Learning: A Survey on Privacy-Preserving Collaborative Intelligence
item.description:
Summary

  • Comprehensive survey of federated learning (FL): architecture, lifecycle (local training, aggregation, global updates), and communication protocols.
  • Discusses key technical challenges in FL such as non-IID data, system/hardware heterogeneity, and communication efficiency.
  • Covers privacy-preserving mechanisms (e.g., differential privacy, secure aggregation) and related techniques to protect training data during FL.
  • Surveys emerging trends (personalized FL, cross-device vs cross-silo), applications, benchmarks, evaluation metrics, and open research directions.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ratun Rahman']
  • Tags: ['federated-learning', 'privacy', 'differential-privacy', 'secure-aggregation', 'survey']

item.title: SAHOO: Safeguarded Alignment for High-Order Optimization Objectives in Recursive Self-Improvement
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SAHOO, a framework of three safeguards to prevent alignment drift during recursive self-improvement: (i) Goal Drift Index (GDI) — a learned multi-signal detector, (ii) constraint preservation checks for safety-critical invariants, and (iii) regression-risk quantification to flag harmful improvement cycles.
  • Empirical evaluation on 189 tasks (code generation, mathematical reasoning, truthfulness) shows substantial quality gains (e.g., +18.3% on code, +16.8% on reasoning) while preserving constraints and maintaining low truthfulness violations; thresholds calibrated on a small validation set.
  • Analyzes the capability–alignment frontier, documenting rising alignment costs with further self-improvement and domain-specific tensions (e.g., fluency vs. factuality), making alignment preservation measurable and deployable.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subramanyam Sahoo', 'Aman Chadha', 'Vinija Jain', 'Divya Chaudhary']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'alignment', 'recursive self-improvement', 'safety monitoring', 'robustness']

item.title: Margin and Consistency Supervision for Calibrated and Robust Vision Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Margin and Consistency Supervision (MaCS), a training regularizer combining a hinge-squared margin penalty on logits and a KL-based consistency loss between clean and mildly perturbed views.
  • Provides theoretical analysis linking increased logit margin and reduced local sensitivity to improved generalization and a provable robustness-radius bound that scales with the margin-to-sensitivity ratio.
  • Empirically improves calibration (ECE, NLL) and robustness to common corruptions across multiple image classification benchmarks and backbones (CNNs and ViTs) without extra data or architectural changes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Salim Khazem']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'calibration', 'consistency-regularization', 'margin-regularization', 'vision']

item.title: Knowing without Acting: The Disentangled Geometry of Safety Mechanisms in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the Disentangled Safety Hypothesis (DSH): safety is split into a Recognition Axis (Knowing) and an Execution Axis (Acting), which disentangle across model layers.
  • Introduces Double-Difference Extraction and Adaptive Causal Steering to causally demonstrate a double dissociation yielding 'Knowing without Acting.'
  • Develops the Refusal Erasure Attack (REA) that surgically disables the refusal mechanism to achieve state-of-the-art jailbreak success.
  • Analyzes architectural differences in safety control (explicit semantic control in Llama3.1 vs latent distributed control in Qwen2.5) and provides code/dataset.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jinman Wu', 'Yi Xie', 'Shen Lin', 'Shiqian Zhao', 'Xiaofeng Chen']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'model-internals', 'causal-intervention', 'safety-vulnerability', 'red-teaming']

item.title: From Decoupled to Coupled: Robustness Verification for Learning-based Keypoint Detection with Joint Specifications
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a coupled robustness verification framework for heatmap-based keypoint detectors that bounds joint deviations across all keypoints, capturing interdependencies and downstream task requirements.
  • Formulates verification as a falsification problem solved via a mixed-integer linear program (MILP) that combines reachable heatmap sets with a polytope encoding of joint deviation constraints; infeasibility yields a sound robustness certificate, feasibility yields counterexamples.
  • Empirical results show higher verified rates and effectiveness under strict error thresholds where prior decoupled (per-keypoint) verification methods are overly conservative or fail.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xusheng Luo', 'Changliu Liu']
  • Tags: ['robustness verification', 'formal verification', 'adversarial robustness', 'MILP', 'computer vision']

item.title: Information-Theoretic Privacy Control for Sequential Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes compositional privacy leakage in sequential multi-agent LLM pipelines using mutual information and derives theoretical bounds on how local leakage can amplify across agents.
  • Proposes a privacy-regularized training framework that constrains information flow between agent outputs and agent-local sensitive variables to control system-level leakage.
  • Empirically evaluates the method across pipelines of varying depth on benchmarks, showing stable optimization and interpretable privacy–utility trade-offs, and argues local constraints alone are insufficient.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sadia Asif', 'Mohammad Mohammadi Amiri']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'information-theoretic', 'multi-agent-systems', 'privacy-preserving-training']

item.title: When One Modality Rules Them All: Backdoor Modality Collapse in Multimodal Diffusion Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and defines 'Backdoor Modality Collapse' where multimodal backdoors degenerate to rely primarily on a subset of modalities rather than synergistically on all.
  • Introduces two metrics—Trigger Modality Attribution (TMA) and Cross-Trigger Interaction (CTI)—to quantify modality-specific contributions and interactions of backdoors.
  • Presents extensive experiments on multimodal conditional diffusion models showing a consistent winner-takes-all dynamic, negligible or negative cross-modal interaction, and implications for defenses and evaluation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qitong Wang', 'Haoran Dai', 'Haotian Zhang', 'Christopher Rasmussen', 'Binghui Wang']
  • Tags: ['backdoor', 'multimodal security', 'diffusion models', 'attack analysis', 'robustness metrics']

item.title: Revisiting the (Sub)Optimality of Best-of-N for Inference-Time Alignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Reanalyzes Best-of-N (BoN) sampling for inference-time alignment of language models using win-rate (pairwise comparison) as the evaluation objective instead of expected true reward.
  • Shows that under realistic assumptions about the reference and learned reward models, properly tuned BoN is both computationally and statistically optimal for maximizing win-rate, explaining its empirical success.
  • Identifies that BoN remains vulnerable to reward-hacking in this setting and introduces a simple practical variant that provably eliminates reward-hacking while preserving optimal statistical performance.
  • Proves that several prior approaches are provably suboptimal when measured by win-rate, emphasizing the need to choose appropriate objectives in alignment analyses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ved Sriraman', 'Adam Block']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'inference-time alignment', 'adversarial exploitation', 'defense mechanism']

item.title: Identifying Adversary Characteristics from an Observed Attack
item.description:
Summary

  • Proves attacker non-identifiability from an observed attack without additional knowledge (multiple attackers can produce the same attack).
  • Proposes a domain-agnostic framework to identify the most probable attacker given an observed attack and (implicit) prior information.
  • Argues that attacker characterization aids defense via exogenous mitigation and improves defenses that modify the learning process (e.g., adversarial regularization).
  • Demonstrates framework instantiations across multiple learner types to illustrate applicability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Soyon Choi', 'Scott Alfeld', 'Meiyi Ma']
  • Tags: ['adversarial ML', 'attacker attribution', 'defense mechanisms', 'security analysis']

item.title: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Traversal-as-Policy: distill execution logs into a single executable Gated Behavior Tree (GBT) that externalizes long-horizon policy and enforces deterministic pre-execution gates derived from unsafe traces.
  • GBT traversal matches model intent to mined action macros, executes guarded macros one at a time, maintains a compact spine memory instead of transcript replay, and performs risk-aware recovery when stalled.
  • Design emphasizes safety and verifiability (monotonic gate updates that prevent re-admission of previously unsafe contexts) and efficiency (reduced token/character usage).
  • Evaluated across 15+ software, web, reasoning, and safety/security benchmarks showing large gains in success rate and dramatic reductions in violations (e.g., SWE-bench Verified: success 34.6%→73.6%, violations 2.8%→0.2%).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Peiran Li', 'Jiashuo Sun', 'Fangzhou Lin', 'Shuo Xing', 'Tianfu Fu', 'Suofei Feng', 'Chaoqun Ni', 'Zhengzhong Tu']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'safe agents', 'policy distillation', 'behavior trees', 'verification']

item.title: vLLM Semantic Router: Signal Driven Decision Routing for Mixture-of-Modality Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents vLLM Semantic Router, a signal-driven routing framework that extracts heterogeneous signals (heuristics and neural classifiers) from requests and composes them via configurable Boolean decision rules to select the best model endpoint.
  • Includes per-decision plugin chains that enforce privacy and safety constraints such as jailbreak detection, PII filtering, and hallucination detection (HaluGate), enabling guardrails at routing time.
  • Supports multi-provider, multi-endpoint deployments (OpenAI, Anthropic, vLLM, cloud providers) and expresses deployment-specific policies (cost, latency, privacy) without code changes; implemented as an Envoy external processor in production.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xunzhuo Liu', 'Huamin Chen', 'Samzong Lu', 'Yossi Ovadia', 'Guohong Wen', 'Hao Wu', 'Zhengda Tan', 'Jintao Zhang', 'Senan Zedan', 'Yehudit Kerido', 'Liav Weiss', 'Haichen Zhang', 'Bishen Yu', 'Asaad Balum', 'Noa Limoy', 'Abdallah Samara', 'Baofa Fan', 'Brent Salisbury', 'Ryan Cook', 'Zhijie Wang', 'Qiping Pan', 'Rehan Khan', 'Avishek Goswami', 'Houston H. Zhang', 'Shuyi Wang', 'Ziang Tang', 'Fang Han', 'Zohaib Hassan', 'Jianqiao Zheng', 'Avinash Changrani']
  • Tags: ['safety/guardrails', 'jailbreak detection', 'PII/privacy filtering', 'model routing/security middleware']

item.title: Fragile Thoughts: How Large Language Models Handle Chain-of-Thought Perturbations
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a taxonomy of 5 Chain-of-Thought (CoT) perturbation types (MathError, UnitConversion, Sycophancy, SkippedSteps, ExtraSteps) and injects these perturbations into intermediate reasoning steps.
  • Empirically evaluates 13 LLMs (≈3B to 1.5T parameters) on mathematical reasoning tasks to measure robustness to these perturbations and where in the chain they are injected.
  • Finds heterogeneous vulnerability patterns: MathError harms small models most but improves with scale; UnitConversion remains challenging across scales; ExtraSteps have minimal effect; Sycophancy and SkippedSteps cause modest-to-intermediate degradation.
  • Reports scaling relationships (power-law) and emphasizes implications for multi-stage reasoning pipelines, highlighting need for task-specific robustness assessments and mitigation strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ashwath Vaithinathan Aravindan', 'Mayank Kejriwal']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'adversarial-perturbation', 'chain-of-thought', 'evaluation', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Peak + Accumulation: A Proxy-Level Scoring Formula for Multi-Turn LLM Attack Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Peak + Accumulation, a proxy-level scoring formula that combines peak per-turn risk, persistence ratio, and category diversity to detect multi-turn prompt injection attacks without invoking an LLM.
  • Identifies limitations of weighted-average aggregation and motivates the approach with analogies to CUSUM change-point detection, Bayesian belief updating, and security alerting; includes a sensitivity analysis showing a phase transition at persistence rho ≈ 0.4.
  • Evaluates on 10,654 conversations (588 attack, 10,066 benign) achieving 90.8% recall at 1.20% false positive rate (F1 85.9%), and releases the scoring algorithm, pattern library, and evaluation harness as open source.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['J Alex Corll']
  • Tags: ['prompt-injection', 'multi-turn-attacks', 'attack-detection', 'proxy-layer-defense']

item.title: Window-based Membership Inference Attacks Against Fine-tuned Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces WBC (Window-Based Comparison), a sliding-window, sign-based aggregation attack for membership inference against fine-tuned LLMs that compares token-sequence losses between target and reference models.
  • Ensembles binary votes from geometrically spaced window sizes to capture memorization signals from token-level to phrase-level, rather than relying on global-average loss.
  • Evaluates across eleven datasets, showing substantial improvements in AUC and 2–3x better detection rates at low false-positive thresholds, revealing privacy vulnerabilities in fine-tuned LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuetian Chen', 'Yuntao Du', 'Kaiyuan Zhang', 'Ashish Kundu', 'Charles Fleming', 'Bruno Ribeiro', 'Ninghui Li']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'privacy', 'attack', 'LLM memorization', 'adversarial evaluation']

item.title: Agent Tools Orchestration Leaks More: Dataset, Benchmark, and Mitigation
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a new privacy vulnerability (Tools Orchestration Privacy Risk, TOP-R) where multi-tool LLM agents aggregate non-sensitive fragments from different tools to infer sensitive information.
  • Provides a formal characterization of TOP-R (three necessary conditions), constructs TOP-Bench via a RISE pipeline with paired social-context scenarios, and introduces the H-Score to measure utility-safety trade-offs.
  • Empirically evaluates six state-of-the-art LLMs showing high leakage rates, analyzes root causes (privacy awareness gaps, reasoning overshoot, inference inertia), and proposes mitigation strategies; the best configuration (Dual-Constraint Privacy Enhancement) substantially improves H-Score.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuxuan Qiao', 'Dongqin Liu', 'Hongchang Yang', 'Wei Zhou', 'Songlin Hu']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'autonomous-agents', 'dataset', 'benchmark', 'defenses']

item.title: Reasoned Safety Alignment: Ensuring Jailbreak Defense via Answer-Then-Check
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Answer-Then-Check (Reasoned Safety Alignment, ReSA): models first generate an answer 'in thought' then evaluate its safety before producing a final response, aimed at mitigating jailbreaks.
  • Introduces the ReSA dataset (80K samples) for training this safety reasoning behavior and shows fine-tuned models reach a strong safety-vs-utility Pareto frontier with reduced over-refusal.
  • Evaluations indicate the method preserves general reasoning (MMLU, MATH500, HumanEval), outperforms post-hoc detectors for safe completion, and can be data-efficient (≈500 samples can approach full-data performance).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chentao Cao', 'Xiaojun Xu', 'Bo Han', 'Hang Li']
  • Tags: ['jailbreak defense', 'safety alignment', 'safety dataset', 'robustness training']

item.title: Federated Learning: A Survey on Privacy-Preserving Collaborative Intelligence
item.description:
Summary

  • Comprehensive survey of federated learning (FL): architecture, lifecycle (local training, aggregation, global updates), and communication protocols.
  • Discusses key technical challenges in FL such as non-IID data, system/hardware heterogeneity, and communication efficiency.
  • Covers privacy-preserving mechanisms (e.g., differential privacy, secure aggregation) and related techniques to protect training data during FL.
  • Surveys emerging trends (personalized FL, cross-device vs cross-silo), applications, benchmarks, evaluation metrics, and open research directions.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ratun Rahman']
  • Tags: ['federated-learning', 'privacy', 'differential-privacy', 'secure-aggregation', 'survey']

item.title: Sysformer: Safeguarding Frozen Large Language Models with Adaptive System Prompts
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Sysformer, a transformer that adapts the system prompt in the LLM input embedding space while keeping the target LLM frozen.
  • Trains Sysformer to steer responses toward refusal on harmful prompts and correct replies on safe prompts, improving safety without fine-tuning the LLM.
  • Evaluates on 5 LLMs and 2 benchmarks, showing large gains in refusal rates on harmful prompts and improved compliance on safe prompts; robust against various jailbreaking attacks.
  • Aims to provide a cheaper, deployable defense (adaptive system prompts) for safeguarding instruction-tuned LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kartik Sharma', 'Yiqiao Jin', 'Vineeth Rakesh', 'Yingtong Dou', 'Menghai Pan', 'Mahashweta Das', 'Srijan Kumar']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'jailbreaking', 'prompt-based safety', 'robustness', 'model hardening']

item.title: ContextBench: Modifying Contexts for Targeted Latent Activation
item.description:
Summary

  • Formalizes 'context modification' as a class of methods to generate linguistically fluent inputs that activate specific latent features or elicit target behaviours in language models.
  • Introduces ContextBench, a benchmark to assess elicitation strength and linguistic fluency for targeted activation and safety-related use cases.
  • Evaluates state-of-the-art methods, showing trade-offs between activation effectiveness and fluency.
  • Proposes enhanced Evolutionary Prompt Optimisation (EPO) variants using LLM assistance and diffusion-model inpainting, achieving improved balance between elicitation and fluency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Robert Graham', 'Edward Stevinson', 'Leo Richter', 'Alexander Chia', 'Joseph Miller', 'Joseph Isaac Bloom']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'targeted-elicitation', 'benchmarking', 'model-safety', 'jailbreaking']

item.title: ESAA-Security: An Event-Sourced, Verifiable Architecture for Agent-Assisted Security Audits of AI-Generated Code
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ESAA-Security, an event-sourced architecture that makes agent-assisted security audits of (especially AI-generated) code traceable, reproducible, and verifiable via append-only events, constrained outputs, and replay-based verification.
  • Structures the audit workflow into four phases (reconnaissance, domain audit execution, risk classification, final reporting) with 26 tasks, 16 security domains, and 95 executable checks, producing structured findings, severity classifications, remediation guidance, and auditable reports.
  • Implements governance and defenses for agentic workflows by separating heuristic agent cognition from deterministic state mutation, validating agent intents, persisting accepted outputs, and verifying consistency through replay and hashing.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Elzo Brito dos Santos Filho']
  • Tags: ['security-audit', 'agent-systems', 'verifiable-logs', 'LLM-safety', 'audit-automation']

item.title: Do Compact SSL Backbones Matter for Audio Deepfake Detection? A Controlled Study with RAPTOR
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents RAPTOR, a pairwise-gated fusion detector, to evaluate compact SSL backbones (HuBERT, WavLM) for audio deepfake detection across 14 cross-domain benchmarks.
  • Finds that multilingual HuBERT pre-training, rather than model scale, primarily drives cross-domain robustness—allowing ~100M models to match larger/commercial systems.
  • Introduces a test-time augmentation protocol using perturbation-based aleatoric uncertainty to reveal calibration differences not visible in standard metrics.
  • Reports that WavLM variants show overconfident miscalibration under perturbations, while iterative mHuBERT remains more stable, highlighting pre-training trajectory impact on reliable detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ajinkya Kulkarni', 'Sandipana Dowerah', 'Atharva Kulkarni', 'Tanel Alum\\"ae', 'Mathew Magimai Doss']
  • Tags: ['audio-deepfake-detection', 'robustness', 'self-supervised-learning', 'calibration', 'test-time-augmentation']

item.title: Sensitivity-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Intent Clarification
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the problem of retrieval-augmented intent clarification in sensitive domains and frames the need for the agent to act as a mediator/gatekeeper for protected collections.
  • Outlines a three-step research agenda: (1) define an attack model targeting retrieval-augmented clarification, (2) design sensitivity-aware defenses at the retrieval level, and (3) develop evaluation methods to quantify protection vs. utility trade-offs.
  • Focuses on privacy/security of retrieved data in conversational search (e.g., healthcare, legal, FOIA) rather than general capability improvements of LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Position

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Maik Larooij']
  • Tags: ['sensitive-data', 'private-retrieval', 'attack-model', 'defense-mechanisms', 'evaluation/benchmarking']

item.title: Restoring Linguistic Grounding in VLA Models via Train-Free Attention Recalibration
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a failure mode in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models called "linguistic blindness", where models ignore contradictory language instructions and follow visual priors.
  • Introduces ICBench, a diagnostic benchmark that injects out-of-distribution contradictory instructions into LIBERO tasks to probe language-action coupling.
  • Finds that multiple representative VLA architectures often execute visually plausible actions despite logically impossible instructions.
  • Proposes IGAR (Instruction-Guided Attention Recalibration), a train-free, inference-time mechanism that rebalances attention to restore instruction influence; demonstrates reduced erroneous executions across 30 tasks and on a real Franka robot.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ninghao Zhang', 'Bin Zhu', 'Shijie Zhou', 'Jingjing Chen']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'safety', 'benchmarks', 'inference-time defense', 'vision-language-action']

item.title: BlackMirror: Black-Box Backdoor Detection for Text-to-Image Models via Instruction-Response Deviation
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BlackMirror, a training-free, black-box framework to detect backdoors in text-to-image models.
  • Introduces MirrorMatch to align visual patterns with instructions for detecting semantic deviations, and MirrorVerify to test the stability of those deviations across varied prompts.
  • Designed as a plug-and-play module for Model-as-a-Service deployments and evaluated across diverse backdoor attacks, including cases with visually diverse triggered generations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Feiran Li', 'Qianqian Xu', 'Shilong Bao', 'Zhiyong Yang', 'Xilin Zhao', 'Xiaochun Cao', 'Qingming Huang']
  • Tags: ['backdoor-detection', 'black-box', 'text-to-image', 'model-security', 'defense']

item.title: Margin and Consistency Supervision for Calibrated and Robust Vision Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Margin and Consistency Supervision (MaCS), a training regularizer combining a hinge-squared margin penalty on logits and a KL-based consistency loss between clean and mildly perturbed views.
  • Provides theoretical analysis linking increased logit margin and reduced local sensitivity to improved generalization and a provable robustness-radius bound that scales with the margin-to-sensitivity ratio.
  • Empirically improves calibration (ECE, NLL) and robustness to common corruptions across multiple image classification benchmarks and backbones (CNNs and ViTs) without extra data or architectural changes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Salim Khazem']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'calibration', 'consistency-regularization', 'margin-regularization', 'vision']

item.title: Proof-of-Guardrail in AI Agents and What (Not) to Trust from It
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Proof-of-Guardrail: a system using Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) to produce cryptographic attestations that a specific open-source guardrail ran when generating an agent response.
  • Implements the approach for OpenClaw agents and measures latency overhead and deployment costs.
  • Maintains developer model privacy while ensuring integrity of guardrail execution, and discusses practical limitations and attack scenarios (e.g., malicious developers actively jailbreaking or deceiving attestations).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xisen Jin', 'Michael Duan', 'Qin Lin', 'Aaron Chan', 'Zhenglun Chen', 'Junyi Du', 'Xiang Ren']
  • Tags: ['guardrails', 'TEE/attestation', 'verifiable safety', 'jailbreaking/attack analysis', 'deployment evaluation']

item.title: Knowing without Acting: The Disentangled Geometry of Safety Mechanisms in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the Disentangled Safety Hypothesis (DSH): safety is split into a Recognition Axis (Knowing) and an Execution Axis (Acting), which disentangle across model layers.
  • Introduces Double-Difference Extraction and Adaptive Causal Steering to causally demonstrate a double dissociation yielding 'Knowing without Acting.'
  • Develops the Refusal Erasure Attack (REA) that surgically disables the refusal mechanism to achieve state-of-the-art jailbreak success.
  • Analyzes architectural differences in safety control (explicit semantic control in Llama3.1 vs latent distributed control in Qwen2.5) and provides code/dataset.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jinman Wu', 'Yi Xie', 'Shen Lin', 'Shiqian Zhao', 'Xiaofeng Chen']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'model-internals', 'causal-intervention', 'safety-vulnerability', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Revisiting the (Sub)Optimality of Best-of-N for Inference-Time Alignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Reanalyzes Best-of-N (BoN) sampling for inference-time alignment of language models using win-rate (pairwise comparison) as the evaluation objective instead of expected true reward.
  • Shows that under realistic assumptions about the reference and learned reward models, properly tuned BoN is both computationally and statistically optimal for maximizing win-rate, explaining its empirical success.
  • Identifies that BoN remains vulnerable to reward-hacking in this setting and introduces a simple practical variant that provably eliminates reward-hacking while preserving optimal statistical performance.
  • Proves that several prior approaches are provably suboptimal when measured by win-rate, emphasizing the need to choose appropriate objectives in alignment analyses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ved Sriraman', 'Adam Block']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'inference-time alignment', 'adversarial exploitation', 'defense mechanism']

item.title: SecureRAG-RTL: A Retrieval-Augmented, Multi-Agent, Zero-Shot LLM-Driven Framework for Hardware Vulnerability Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SecureRAG-RTL, a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework that combines domain-specific retrieval with LLM-based generative reasoning to detect security vulnerabilities in hardware description language (HDL) designs.
  • Establishes prompt-only baselines and shows SecureRAG-RTL improves vulnerability detection accuracy by ~30% across multiple LLM architectures and sizes.
  • Curates and annotates a benchmark dataset of 14 HDL designs with real-world security vulnerabilities and plans public release to support future research.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Touseef Hasan', 'Blessing Airehenbuwa', 'Nitin Pundir', 'Souvika Sarkar', 'Ujjwal Guin']
  • Tags: ['hardware security', 'vulnerability detection', 'retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)', 'LLM-driven security verification', 'benchmark/dataset']

item.title: The Fragility Of Moral Judgment In Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a perturbation framework to test stability/manipulability of LLM moral judgments using 2,939 real-world dilemmas and 129,156 model judgments across four LLMs.
  • Evaluates three perturbation families—surface edits, point-of-view shifts, and persuasion cues—and multiple evaluation protocols to measure flip rates and directional biases.
  • Finds surface noise yields low flip rates (~7.5%), POV shifts cause substantial instability (~24.3%), persuasion cues produce systematic directional shifts, and protocol choices (prompt scaffolding/order) dominate reproducibility.
  • Shows instability concentrates in morally ambiguous cases and warns that narrative form and presentation skill can co-produce moral outcomes, raising robustness, fairness, and reproducibility concerns.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tom van Nuenen', 'Pratik S. Sachdeva']
  • Tags: ['prompt-manipulation', 'adversarial-robustness', 'model-manipulation', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Post Fusion Bird's Eye View Feature Stabilization for Robust Multimodal 3D Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Post Fusion Stabilizer (PFS), a lightweight module that refines intermediate bird's-eye view (BEV) features from existing camera-LiDAR fusion 3D detectors to improve robustness to domain shift and sensor corruptions.
  • PFS stabilizes feature statistics, suppresses regions affected by sensor degradation, and uses residual correction to restore weakened cues while acting as a near-identity transformation to preserve baseline performance.
  • Demonstrates improved robustness on nuScenes under various failure modes (e.g., +1.2% camera dropout robustness, +4.4% mAP in low-light) with a small parameter overhead (~3.3M).
  • Designed to be a drop-in post-fusion module that does not require modifying or retraining the original fusion architecture.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Trung Tien Dong', 'Dev Thakkar', 'Arman Sargolzaei', 'Xiaomin Lin']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'sensor-failure tolerance', 'multimodal fusion', 'autonomous driving', 'BEV feature stabilization']

item.title: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Traversal-as-Policy: distill execution logs into a single executable Gated Behavior Tree (GBT) that externalizes long-horizon policy and enforces deterministic pre-execution gates derived from unsafe traces.
  • GBT traversal matches model intent to mined action macros, executes guarded macros one at a time, maintains a compact spine memory instead of transcript replay, and performs risk-aware recovery when stalled.
  • Design emphasizes safety and verifiability (monotonic gate updates that prevent re-admission of previously unsafe contexts) and efficiency (reduced token/character usage).
  • Evaluated across 15+ software, web, reasoning, and safety/security benchmarks showing large gains in success rate and dramatic reductions in violations (e.g., SWE-bench Verified: success 34.6%→73.6%, violations 2.8%→0.2%).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Peiran Li', 'Jiashuo Sun', 'Fangzhou Lin', 'Shuo Xing', 'Tianfu Fu', 'Suofei Feng', 'Chaoqun Ni', 'Zhengzhong Tu']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'safe agents', 'policy distillation', 'behavior trees', 'verification']

item.title: SAHOO: Safeguarded Alignment for High-Order Optimization Objectives in Recursive Self-Improvement
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SAHOO, a framework of three safeguards to prevent alignment drift during recursive self-improvement: (i) Goal Drift Index (GDI) — a learned multi-signal detector, (ii) constraint preservation checks for safety-critical invariants, and (iii) regression-risk quantification to flag harmful improvement cycles.
  • Empirical evaluation on 189 tasks (code generation, mathematical reasoning, truthfulness) shows substantial quality gains (e.g., +18.3% on code, +16.8% on reasoning) while preserving constraints and maintaining low truthfulness violations; thresholds calibrated on a small validation set.
  • Analyzes the capability–alignment frontier, documenting rising alignment costs with further self-improvement and domain-specific tensions (e.g., fluency vs. factuality), making alignment preservation measurable and deployable.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subramanyam Sahoo', 'Aman Chadha', 'Vinija Jain', 'Divya Chaudhary']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'alignment', 'recursive self-improvement', 'safety monitoring', 'robustness']

item.title: Reasoning Models Struggle to Control their Chains of Thought
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CoT-Control, an evaluation suite to measure chain-of-thought (CoT) controllability: whether models can follow instructions about what to verbalize in their CoT while solving tasks (e.g., solve a genetics question without using the word 'chromosome').
  • Empirical results show substantially lower CoT controllability than final-output controllability (example: Claude Sonnet 4.5 — 2.7% CoT controllability vs 61.9% output controllability).
  • Finds controllability rises with model size but falls with more RL training, greater test-time compute, and higher problem difficulty; controllability remains low even under incentives to evade monitoring or adversarial prompt optimization, with only a small boost when models are told they are monitored.
  • Concludes CoT monitorability is unlikely to be currently compromised but recommends tracking CoT controllability in future models; mechanism behind low controllability is not yet understood.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chen Yueh-Han', 'Robert McCarthy', 'Bruce W. Lee', 'He He', 'Ian Kivlichan', 'Bowen Baker', 'Micah Carroll', 'Tomek Korbak']
  • Tags: ['chain-of-thought', 'monitorability', 'adversarial-evasion', 'benchmarking', 'model-robustness']
